behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in template-lint logic itself (string/rule detection), not a module handoff. REAL-INFRA: none required. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly says "static prompt-template lint check" and does not require harness assembly or file-loading side effects.
behaviors.test_description: Test the lint function with in-memory template strings, including positive and negative fixtures that contain phase instructions copied from SKILL.md text; assert rejection/acceptance outcomes.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears at module boundaries (template + interpolation + fixture expansion + final assembly). Unit is too low because isolated lint logic can pass while assembled prompt leaks. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required beyond local in-process wiring. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System with quoted disqualifier "fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness" (assembly, not external infra behavior).
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-construction pipeline with representative fixtures and interpolation variables, then assert the final prompt text excludes phase-specific SKILL.md instruction snippets.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is about wiring across loader, file-backed skill context, and invocation path; Unit is too low. REAL-INFRA: local files are sufficient; no subprocess/network requirement. LLM-DEP: this behavior can be validated by source-of-truth provenance checks and controlled outputs, not model quality. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Agentic because the quoted behavior focuses on "loads skill context from plugin/skill files" and "rather than hard-coded prompt rules" (provenance/orchestration).
behaviors.test_description: Execute invocation using real skill-loader modules against fixture plugin/skill files; assert the active context originates from file content and that removing hard-coded prompt rules does not change sourced context.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires harness/file-variant orchestration; Unit is too low. REAL-INFRA: local fixture file swapping is enough; no real subprocess/network needed. LLM-DEP: behavior is capability to run unchanged prompt across variants, not semantic model correctness. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Agentic because quoted scope is "can run ... without changing test prompt text" (execution-path invariance).
behaviors.test_description: Parameterize harness inputs with normal, degraded, and missing SKILL.md fixtures while keeping identical user-style prompt text; assert invocation succeeds/fails in expected harness-controlled way and prompt text is unchanged.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: pass/fail depends on whether model behavior changes with degraded skill context; deterministic stubs can hide real failures. MOCK-HIDE: a mocked model could still return expected structure and falsely pass. MULTI-STEP: only one model decision boundary is required by behavior text, so Workflow not required. Rejected Integration because quoted requirement is behavioral failure "under the same user-style prompt" due SKILL.md degradation, i.e., model decision/content sensitivity.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model tests with identical user-style prompt against baseline vs degraded SKILL.md context; assert baseline passes and degraded variant fails behavior assertions.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering across modules (model-call wrapper, capture writer, parser) is the failure boundary; Unit may miss pipeline sequencing defects. REAL-INFRA: local artifact write is enough; no real network/subprocess requirement for this invariant. LLM-DEP: can use deterministic stubbed model text; not dependent on model quality. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because behavior text is about internal ordering "before any JSON parsing" not OS-specific failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the response-processing pipeline with a controlled model-output stub; assert diagnostic artifact is written with raw text prior to invoking parse/extraction functions (e.g., via timestamps/call-order hooks).

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core failure is assertion logic strength (structure vs keywords), which can be validated in a local assertion module. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration because quoted behavior targets assertion semantics "not only keyword presence checks," which can be proven with crafted in-memory payload fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Test assertion helpers with synthetic outputs where keywords exist but structure is invalid (wrong schema paths/roles) and with valid structured outputs; assert structural checks fail/pass accordingly.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires "live model execution" and compares real output behaviors; correctness depends on actual model-produced content/structure. MOCK-HIDE: keyword-only false positives vs structural failures are only meaningful on real model outputs. MULTI-STEP: still a single response-evaluation boundary, so Workflow not strictly required. Rejected Integration due quoted disqualifier "Under live model execution"; rejected Workflow because behavior does not require multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model smoke tests, evaluate both keyword-only and structural assertions on the same outputs, and confirm at least one regression case where keyword-only passes but structural assertions fail.
